title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people who share their experiences of living in or moving to a city. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Marta, 29, Architect
Moving to the city was a huge adjustment for me. I grew up in a small town, so [43]the constant noise and fast pace were overwhelming at first[/43]. Over time, I learned to appreciate the endless opportunities here. I love that I can walk to art galleries or try food from around the world. However, [47]I sometimes miss the sense of community I had back home[/47]. Making friends in the city takes more effort, but I’ve found joining local clubs helps. I wouldn’t trade the excitement of city life, but [50]I do need to escape to the countryside now and then to recharge[/50].
texts.B:
### Liam, 35, Software Developer
I moved to the city for work, and [44]at first, I struggled with the high cost of living[/44]. Rent and transport eat up a lot of my salary, but the career opportunities make it worthwhile. I enjoy the diversity and the fact that [48]there’s always something happening, from concerts to street festivals[/48]. One thing I’ve learned is to [51]plan my journeys carefully to avoid rush hour[/51], otherwise commuting can be exhausting. Despite the challenges, I feel like I belong here now.
texts.C:
### Priya, 24, Student
I was born and raised in the city, so [45]I can’t imagine living anywhere else[/45]. I love the energy and the fact that [49]you meet people from all walks of life[/49]. My friends from smaller towns often say they’d feel lost here, but for me, [52]the city is where I feel most at home[/52]. Of course, it can be stressful, especially during exam season, but I find peace in the city’s parks and quiet cafes.
texts.D:
### Carlos, 41, Chef
I came to the city ten years ago, chasing a dream to open my own restaurant. [46]It wasn’t easy at first—finding affordable space and building a customer base took years[/46]. But the city’s vibrant food scene inspired me to keep going. [47]I love how people here are open to trying new cuisines[/47], and I’ve made friends from all over the world. Sometimes, [49]I miss the slower pace of my hometown[/49], but I wouldn’t change my decision.

questions.question: found the city’s constant activity difficult to get used to?
questions.explanation: Marta describes being overwhelmed by the noise and fast pace at first.

questions.question: was surprised by how expensive city life can be?
questions.explanation: Liam struggled with the high cost of living when he moved.

questions.question: cannot imagine living anywhere but the city?
questions.explanation: Priya says she was born and raised in the city and can’t imagine living elsewhere.

questions.question: had to overcome significant obstacles to start a business in the city?
questions.explanation: Carlos describes the difficulties of opening his restaurant.

questions.question: sometimes misses the close-knit feeling of a smaller community?
questions.explanation: Marta misses the sense of community from her hometown.

questions.question: enjoys the variety of events always happening in the city?
questions.explanation: Liam mentions there’s always something happening, like concerts and festivals.

questions.question: appreciates meeting people from many different backgrounds?
questions.explanation: Priya enjoys meeting people from all walks of life.

questions.question: needs to leave the city occasionally to relax?
questions.explanation: Marta says she escapes to the countryside to recharge.

questions.question: has learned to avoid busy travel times in the city?
questions.explanation: Liam plans his journeys to avoid rush hour.

questions.question: feels most comfortable in the city environment?
questions.explanation: Priya says the city is where she feels most at home.
